facts.text: The Historic Campbell House in Okahumpka is listed on the National Register of Historic Places, preserving a piece of the town’s early architecture.
The Okahumpka Rosenwald School, recently added to the National Register, is restored by a local foundation to serve as a historic site and community hub.
Located in western Lake County, Okahumpka is part of the Orlando–Kissimmee Metropolitan Statistical Area, linking it to the greater Central Florida region.
County Road 470 runs west from Okahumpka to an interchange with Florida’s Turnpike, providing a direct route to the state’s major highway system.
The town’s Rosenwald School was built under a philanthropic program that erected over 5,300 schools for Black children across the South in the early 20th century.
The Okahumpka Community Center hosts events that celebrate the town’s century‑long history and community stories.
